prerequisites: A SonarQube Cloud (formerly SonarCloud) account with access to the organization you want to read from.
The organization key for your SonarQube Cloud organization (the `organization` config field).
One or more project keys to extract, supplied as the `component_keys` config field.
A user access token generated from your SonarQube Cloud account, supplied as the `user_token` secret field.

authMethods.name: User token (Bearer)
authMethods.summary: Authentication uses the `user_token` secret field. SonarQube Cloud Web API requests are authenticated with the bearer authentication scheme, sending the token in an `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. Generate the token from your account's My Account > Access Tokens page.

setupSteps.title: Generate a user access token
setupSteps.body: Sign in to SonarQube Cloud, open the account menu in the top right corner and select My Account > Access Tokens. The Personal Tokens tab lists your existing tokens. In Generate Tokens, enter a token name and select Generate Token. Copy the displayed token value immediately, because you will not be able to retrieve it again after you dismiss the notification or leave the page. Provide this value as the `user_token` field.

setupSteps.title: Identify your organization key
setupSteps.body: Determine the key of the SonarQube Cloud organization you want to read from and set it as the `organization` config field. The organization is the top-level entity that contains your projects.

setupSteps.title: List the projects to extract
setupSteps.body: Set the `component_keys` config field to the project key(s) you want to sync. In the SonarQube Cloud Web API, a project is referenced by its component/project key (for example `my_project_key`).

setupSteps.title: Optionally bound the time window
setupSteps.body: Set the optional `start_date` and `end_date` config fields to limit the date range of records pulled for time-based streams. Leave them empty to use the connector defaults.

setupSteps.title: Verify connectivity
setupSteps.body: With `user_token`, `organization`, and `component_keys` configured, the connector authenticates against the SonarQube Cloud Web API at https://sonarcloud.io and extracts data for the listed projects.
